title: Validate planner sizing and followup behavior end-to-end
description: Add or update automated tests that cover the planner prompt/template guidance and the scheduler's suppression of duplicate followups when explicit shared tester/documentation/review beads already exist.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the updated planner prompt or planning output expectations for small developer beads and shared followups.
Tests verify that scheduler suppresses auto-followups when planner-provided followups exist in the feature tree and depend on the completed developer bead.
Tests verify backward compatibility for standalone developer beads that should still receive auto-followups.
Relevant existing test suites for planner/scheduler behavior pass.

handoff_summary.completed: Adjusted planner prompt coverage to match the current prompt wording for small developer beads and shared follow-up guidance. Ran `uv run python -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator.OrchestratorTests.test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children tests.test_orchestrator.OrchestratorTests.test_scheduler_still_creates_auto_followups_for_standalone_developer_bead tests.test_orchestrator.OrchestratorTests.test_planner_writes_shared_followups_at_feature_root_with_multi_bead_dependencies tests.test_orchestrator.OrchestratorTests.test_build_planner_prompt_requires_small_developer_beads_and_shared_followups -v`; all 4 tests passed.
handoff_summary.remaining: No unresolved tester-scope findings in this pass.
handoff_summary.risks: Validation covered the targeted planner/scheduler behaviors required by this bead; no broader suite was run per tester guardrails.

execution_history.summary: Added focused planner/scheduler regression tests in tests/test_orchestrator.py. Planner prompt and plan-shape coverage passes, but the new scheduler test shows duplicate auto-followups are still created even when planner-owned shared followups already exist.
Created corrective bead B-422d9d91-corrective for blocked issue
Requeued blocked bead after corrective bead B-422d9d91-corrective completed
Updated the stale planner-prompt assertions in tests/test_orchestrator.py and reran the targeted orchestrator planner/scheduler tests successfully.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Use `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` to target individual test files rather than `discover`. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite with `discover`. Always target the specific module: `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v`. Running `discover` wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.

worker_prompt_context.title: Validate planner sizing and followup behavior end-to-end
worker_prompt_context.description: Add or update automated tests that cover the planner prompt/template guidance and the scheduler's suppression of duplicate followups when explicit shared tester/documentation/review beads already exist.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the updated planner prompt or planning output expectations for small developer beads and shared followups.
Tests verify that scheduler suppresses auto-followups when planner-provided followups exist in the feature tree and depend on the completed developer bead.
Tests verify backward compatibility for standalone developer beads that should still receive auto-followups.
Relevant existing test suites for planner/scheduler behavior pass.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated tests/test_orchestrator.py with coverage for planner sizing guidance, planner-written shared followups at the feature root, standalone developer auto-followup backward compatibility, and scheduler suppression of duplicate followups. Validation run: `uv run python -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator.OrchestratorTests.test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children tests.test_orchestrator.OrchestratorTests.test_scheduler_still_creates_auto_followups_for_standalone_developer_bead tests.test_orchestrator.OrchestratorTests.test_planner_writes_shared_followups_at_feature_root_with_multi_bead_dependencies tests.test_orchestrator.OrchestratorTests.test_build_planner_prompt_requires_small_developer_beads_and_shared_followups -v`. Three tests passed; the duplicate-followup suppression test failed.
handoff_summary.remaining: Fix scheduler followup creation so a completed developer bead under a planner/feature root reuses existing shared tester/documentation/review beads that already depend on that developer, instead of creating legacy child beads like `<developer>-test`, `<developer>-docs`, and `<developer>-review`. Re-run the targeted orchestrator tests after the scheduler change.
handoff_summary.risks: Current scheduler behavior still creates duplicate followup beads for planner-owned shared followups, so multi-bead feature plans can accumulate redundant tester/documentation/review work and incorrect dependency structure.
handoff_summary.next_action: Update scheduler followup suppression logic, then rerun the targeted unittest module for orchestrator planner/scheduler behavior.
handoff_summary.block_reason: `test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children` fails: after completing a developer bead inside a feature tree with explicit shared tester/documentation/review beads already depending on that developer, the scheduler still creates legacy child followups (`<bead>-test`, `<bead>-docs`, `<bead>-review`).

last_agent_result.summary: Updated the stale planner-prompt assertions in tests/test_orchestrator.py and reran the targeted orchestrator planner/scheduler tests successfully.
